column1: # Pinned co-simulation A-vs-B (recomp vs interpreter) baselines — the ratchet.
# The chain hash is the cumulative FNV fold of context-A's full-state hash at
# every T-cycle checkpoint. A recompiler/runtime change that alters guest
# behavior changes this hash; that is the regression signal.
# Regenerate/verify with (exe SHA-locks to its exact ROM; see rom.cfg gotcha):
#   python tools/gbc_cosim.py --exe <target>_test/build/<exe> \
#       --ab-frames <frames> --stride <stride> --expect-chain <chain>
# All rows below produced matched=1 (byte-identical full state at every
# checkpoint) on 2026-07-01, stride=456 (one scanline).
# 2026-07-02: re-pinned tetris + instr_timing after the EI one-instruction-delay
# fix (gb_tick ime_pending counter; EI now sets ime_pending=2). The fix changes
# WHEN IME is enabled, so the full-state chain legitimately changed; A still ==
# B (matched all checkpoints) and blargg instr_timing still Passes. The oracle's
# first divergence advanced 2,266,273 -> 2,266,364 (a PPU/DMA-timing axis).
# megaman_xtreme2 is STALE — its ROM is not staged in this worktree so its chain
# could not be re-measured; the EI-delay fix affects it too. Re-pin once the ROM
# is available (verification will fail against the old value until then).
# target        stride  frames  chain
megaman_xtreme2 456     1000    E2A240E56E7E348E  # STALE (pre-EI-delay); re-pin when ROM staged
instr_timing    456     120     5D103AEB0D3F03DB
